notes: Curated mortgage and housing market headlines for the LoanInCA news page.

items.title: Freddie Mac said the average 30-year fixed mortgage rate was 6.30%
items.summary: Freddie Mac said the average 30-year fixed rate edged up to 6.30% for the week ending April 30 from 6.23% the week before, while purchase applications stayed more than 20% above a year earlier, showing buyers are still active when rates settle near this range.

items.title: March inflation data stayed firm even as incomes and spending increased
items.summary: The Bureau of Economic Analysis said personal income rose 0.6% in March and consumer spending rose 0.9%, while the PCE price index increased 0.7% from February and 3.5% from a year earlier, a combination that can keep mortgage rates sensitive to inflation releases.

items.title: Redfin saw pending sales improve as rates eased from early-April highs
items.summary: Redfin said pending U.S. home sales rose 2.7% from a year earlier in the four weeks ending April 26, new listings rose 2.2%, and the median monthly payment fell 2.2% as the weekly average mortgage rate moved down to 6.23%.

items.title: MBA reported a small weekly pullback in applications, with purchase demand still solid
items.summary: MBA said total mortgage applications fell 1.6% for the week ending April 24, refinance applications dropped 4%, and purchase applications still rose 1% from the prior week and 21% from a year earlier.

items.title: FHFA said national home prices were flat in February
items.summary: FHFA reported that its seasonally adjusted national house price index was unchanged in February and up 1.7% from a year earlier, reinforcing the view that home-price growth is cooling instead of re-accelerating.

items.title: HUD expanded the credit score models allowed for FHA-insured underwriting
items.summary: HUD said FHA will allow VantageScore 4.0 and FICO 10T for FHA-insured mortgage underwriting, while Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are also moving ahead with the same newer score models, a change aimed at expanding borrower access.

items.title: Fannie Mae kept its 2026 mortgage-rate outlook near 6.2%
items.summary: Fannie Mae's April housing forecast still pointed to a 6.2% average 30-year fixed mortgage rate for 2026, about 4.80 million total home sales this year, and roughly $2.34 trillion in single-family mortgage originations.
